behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific details not mentioned in instruction.md: test_options_parsing_intact checks for 'getTruthy' and 'port' near arguments.ptr[1]; test_no_banned_words_in_dns_zig checks banned patterns like 'std.debug.assert', 'std.Thread.Mutex', 'usingnamespace'; test_oxlint_dns_js checks src/js/node/dns.ts and dns.promises.ts; test_prettier_dns_test_files checks test/js/node/dns/ files. None of these file paths or patterns are discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md says 'The issue occurs when processing the second argument to the lookup function' and points to the exact file and struct (Resolver in dns.zig). Combined with the reproducer showing a non-object string argument, it strongly implies the fix is to change the type-check on the second argument. While it doesn't say 'change isCell to isObject', the description is very close to revealing the exact fix location and nature.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_non_object_guard_on_second_arg uses a specific regex pattern 'if\s*\(arguments\.len\s*>\s*1\s+and\s+arguments\.ptr\[1\]\.(is\w+)\(\)' which requires the guard to follow this exact syntactic form. An alternative correct fix (e.g., using a different conditional structure, adding a separate check, or using 'else if') would fail this regex. Also, test_guard_uses_valid_object_method requires the same regex pattern, locking in the implementation approach.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core fail-to-pass tests (test_non_object_guard_on_second_arg, test_guard_uses_valid_object_method) only do regex matching on source text: `re.search(r'if\s*\(arguments\.len...', content)`. No test actually compiles or executes the code. All tests are text-inspection only ('assert X in content', regex searches on file contents).

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check that the guard method is NOT 'isCell' (the original buggy value) and IS a valid object-discriminating method. A stub or no-op wouldn't pass because the original code has 'isCell' which the test explicitly rejects.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The core behavior described in instruction.md (non-object second argument should not crash, should be silently ignored) is tested via the guard check tests that verify the type-checking method is changed from isCell to an object-discriminating method.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves a bug fix in a DNS resolver, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the file 'src/bun.js/api/bun/dns.zig' and the 'Resolver' struct, and states 'The issue occurs when processing the second argument to the lookup function.' This narrows the fix to essentially one line (the guard on the second argument), which is excessive localization.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits task.
